danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies entering arena via passages on four edges
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat phase; player must engage or be surrounded

danger_signals.visual_form: Hordes of enemies visible on screen in single-screen arenas
danger_signals.meaning: High-pressure combat state; danger from all directions

danger_signals.visual_form: End-of-level boss character appears after map indication
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat; progression checkpoint with elevated difficulty

danger_signals.visual_form: No exit doors visible in arena
danger_signals.meaning: Combat must continue; arena not yet cleared

safety_signals.visual_form: One or more exit doors materialize after enemy clearance
safety_signals.meaning: Arena successfully cleared; safe passage to next room available

safety_signals.visual_form: Gift-wrapped prizes and money piles visible on ground
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; safe to collect during lulls in combat
Tactical advantage available; enhanced safety through augmentation

safety_signals.visual_form: Overhead map showing level layout and boss location
safety_signals.meaning: Progression confirmation; player orientation and strategic planning phase

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Central or right-of-center position in arena
Oversees show; voice provides commentary and challenge framing

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types entering in waves
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enter from passages on four arena edges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arena-based single-screen combat spaces; game show aesthetic with prizes and money as collectibles; sadistic host presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players treat combat as performance/entertainment; collecting cash and prizes becomes secondary objective alongside survival

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Continuous enemy waves; multiple simultaneous threats; cash/prize visibility
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players must balance survival with wealth accumulation; competitive scoring between two players rewards higher cash collector

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Exit doors only appear after arena clearance; map displays after first arena completion; boss rooms gated by level progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No backtracking possible; linear forward movement; forced engagement with escalating difficulty

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Bonus content accessibility tied to resource collection
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Keys appear throughout levels; Pleasure Dome level exists as hidden reward
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players incentivized to collect specific items beyond standard combat objectives

environmental_storytelling.example: Visual design references dystopian films RoboCop and The Running Man (1987); establishes oppressive near-future aesthetic
Host verbal interjections ('Total carnage! I love it!', 'Big money! Big prizes!') communicate game show exploitation themes and player objectives
VCRs, toasters, and gift-wrapped boxes establish consumer-culture satire; bikini-clad 'babes' in Pleasure Dome reinforce exploitative entertainment atmosphere
Single-screen combat spaces with four-directional enemy entry simulate gladiatorial spectacle; money and prizes as primary narrative rewards critique game show commodification

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Enter arena in waves via four edge passages; require elimination before exits appear
Positioned in arena; must rotate between movement and attack directions using twin-stick control
Materialize after enemy elimination; present player with choice of next room path
Appears as end-of-level boss; takes direct action against player
Remain stationary on ground; passive resource for player acquisition

notes: Smash TV communicates world-state primarily through gameplay structure rather than visual variation. The dystopian game show setting is narrative framing for mechanically straightforward twin-stick shooter action. Danger signals center on enemy presence and arena clarity, while safety signals manifest as exit availability and resource presence. Faction indicators are minimal; the game emphasizes player vs. environment rather than factional conflict. The world-state (oppressive entertainment spectacle) is conveyed through thematic elements (host commentary, prize items) and structural mechanics (forced progression, score competition) rather than visual environmental variation. Data sourced from Wikipedia article on Smash TV; platform-specific visual details unavailable in provided source.
